item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's exactly what I thought when I read this.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2g-dzkTD6mw

I thought it was a straight ripoff/adaptation at first

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A memetic thing that kills you. Not very original IMO :/

-1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Currently it has two distinct forms, -A and -B, which have near-identicle properties (-B works a bit faster and can pass through touch) but which are entirely different songs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I corrected a typo ('take the form handwritten documents' in the second sentence of the description), but forgot to describe it in the box. Sorry.

As for the SCP? Mmm, I don't care for it. I found it densely worded and hard to follow (I'm not sure if it's always one song, or can take the form of any/many songs, for example), and overall it just seemed a bit like a meme what kills you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I still can't dissassociate this concept from that old bananaphone flash animation.

EDIT: I also still can't spell.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I found this fairly dense and uninteresting, which is a shame since I could potentially connect with having a song stuck in your head.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's what they do, except for releasing him. They infect him, then amnesticize him enough to erase the self-propagating pattern, but not enough to effect his long-term memory. Then they keep him around in case they need him, in case other versions show up. Otherwise they'd have to wait until someone properly awful with the appropriate musical background came along and use him. But what do they do in the meantime? Constantly infect and then cure a string of D-Class to keep the new version on lockdown until an orchestra conductor goes postal? It seems much cheaper to me to hold on to this one guy that can already do what they need.

EDIT: Personnel status modified pursuant to Ethics Committee recommendations.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked this one in its original draft, and the final product really came together. +1'd for great writing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sounds like this rhythm is…

Puts on sunglasses

Infectious.

YEAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAHHHH!!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not really that interesting, however, it is very creative.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In later stages of the disease the severity of the anomalous symptoms increase, leading to an inability to process external sounds, a loss of the ability to cogitate… and finally death

They can't keep him around very long, then, because pretty soon he's going to perceive nothing but the meme and then die. Seems like it would be better if he wrote it down or, i dunno, hummed it into a recorder if you need it for later comparisons. Then you amnesticize him and release. If it's recorded, it's more ethical, easier to contain, and above all else cheaper.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe I'm missing something, but this just comes off as a random memetic thinger what kills you. I was uninterested, -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can honestly say I have been waiting my entire life to see a new Kraito SCP.

Godspeed, sir.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
They need someone with a musical background to transcribe it. And since its source could not be found, D-1677's services were retained in the event of future outbreaks. Good thing too, since a few years later -B showed up.

Or would you rather they'd released him and kidnapped someone else with a musical background? Or perhaps daisy-chain the infection to keep it stored until someone with a musical background commits a capitol crime?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ah, a triumphant return.

I love it when those happen.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Mr. ██████, now labeled D-1677, was taken into custody

Well there went any hope of getting my upvote. I like the rest of it, but I can't stand the idea of kidnapping and D-classing. At least release the poor bastard once he's finished transcribing.
